name: Sacramento (United States)

colors.symbolism: the Sacramento and American Rivers, which meet at the city
the California gold rush, in which Sacramento played a central role as a supply hub
darker blue elements of the design

description: A white field with sweeping blue and gold curved bands evoking river currents and the gold of the gold rush, with the seal of the City of Sacramento centred on the field.
history: Used by the City of Sacramento, the capital of California since 1854, founded at the confluence of the Sacramento and American Rivers in 1849 during the California Gold Rush.

features.symbolism: A white field with curving blue and gold bands evoking the Sacramento and American Rivers and the gold of California's gold rush, with the city seal at the centre.

features.role: Seal of the City of Sacramento
features.symbolism: The municipal seal of Sacramento, capital of California, founded in 1849 at the confluence of the Sacramento and American Rivers and the principal supply town of the California Gold Rush.
